text:
X-POLE A-FRAME Product Page
X-POLE describes the A-FRAME as a portable, height-adjustable, multi-discipline aerial rig for hammocks, silks, hoops, and the X-FLY Flying Pole. The page reports independent load testing and a working-load limit of 140 kilograms or 308 pounds at a 5:1 safety factor.
The product page lists selectable heights from 8 feet (2,450 millimetres) to 11.42 feet (3,480 millimetres). It gives a 47-square-foot footprint at 8 feet and a 95-square-foot footprint at 11.42 feet. The listed product weight is 71 pounds.
The standard product includes a 55-inch top bar, four telescopic legs with feet, two leg connectors, pins, stabilization straps, carry bags, instructions, and additional weight bags; sand is not included. A 78-inch extended top bar is sold separately.
Depending on the apparatus and connection, the page says buyers may also need separately purchased carabiners, swivels, a Terminal 8, span sets, or a top-bar connector. The frame can be used indoors with sufficient clearance or outdoors, but the separate manual directs users not to leave it outdoors and to store it dry.
The site says the frame requires no ceiling or floor fixings. It lists hammocks, silks, hoops, and the X-FLY Flying Pole as supported disciplines.
title: X-POLE A-FRAME
